title: Discover the 10 Best Herbal Creams for Numbness in Hands

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale cream, also known as ginger cream, is a popular remedy for numbness in hands. This is because ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the hands and fingers. When hands are numb, it's often due to poor blood flow or inflammation, which ginger cream can help to alleviate. The cream's active ingredients, including gingerols and shogaols, work to increase blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing numbness. Additionally, ginger's warming properties can help to relax muscles and improve circulation, further reducing numbness and discomfort. Applying Zingiber officinale cream to the affected area can provide relief from numbness and tingling sensations, allowing for a full range of motion and improved dexterity. The cream's natural and non-invasive properties make it a safe and effective treatment option for numbness in hands.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Piper nigrum herbal cream that helps with numbness in hands, first, you need to prepare the black pepper extract. Start by mixing 2 tablespoons of dried and crushed black pepper with 2 cups of water in a saucepan and bring it to a boil. Then, reduce the heat and let it simmer for about 20 minutes or until the liquid reduces to half. After that, let the mixture cool down and strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl. Next, in a separate bowl, mix 1/2 cup of coconut oil or any other carrier oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil. Melt the beeswax in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals, then combine it with the coconut oil and vitamin E oil and mix well. Now, add the black pepper extract to the oil mixture and stir well. Finally, pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool down and solidify before use. To apply, simply massage a small amount of cream onto the numb area on your hand and wait for the numbing sensation to subside.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for numbness in hands

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum cream is a topical treatment that can help relieve numbness in hands. The cream contains capsaicin, a compound found in chili peppers, which works by blocking the pain-causing chemicals in the nerves. When applied to the affected area, capsaicin blocks the nerve endings that send pain signals to the brain, reducing numbness and tingling. This can be especially helpful for people who experience numbness due to conditions like carpal tunnel syndrome, which can cause pressure on the nerves in the wrist. By applying the cream to the affected area, the capsaicin helps to reduce inflammation and promote blood flow, which can also help to relieve numbness and restore feeling to the hands. The cream can be applied several times a day, and its effects can last for several hours, providing long-lasting relief from numbness.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Piper nigrum herbal cream to help with numbness in hands, you will first need to prepare the pepper extract. To do this, dry 20-30 grams of Piper nigrum (black pepper) in a low-temperature oven or by leaving it in a warm place for a few days. Next, grind the dried Piper nigrum into a fine powder using a spice grinder or a mortar and pestle. Then, mix 10 grams of the powder with 100 milliliters of carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a double boiler. Heat the mixture gently for 30 minutes, then let it cool and strain it through a cheesecloth to remove the solids. Once the extract is ready, you can mix it with a base cream, such as shea butter or beeswax, to create a final product. To make the cream, melt 100 grams of the base in a double boiler, then add 20 milliliters of the Piper nigrum extract and mix well. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and harden before applying it to your hands as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba cream can be helpful for numbness in hands because it improves blood flow to the area. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Piper nigrum herbal creams that help with numbness in hands, you need to start by preparing the peppercorn extract. First, take 1/2 cup of peppercorns and soak them in 2 cups of hot water for 30 minutes. Then, let the mixture steep for 2 hours or overnight. After that, strain the liquid and discard the peppercorns. Next, in a small saucepan, mix 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax, and heat them gently until the beeswax melts. Remove the saucepan from the heat and add 2 tablespoons of the peppercorn extract and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil to it. Stir well and let the mixture cool down. Once it cools and thickens, transfer it to a jar and store it in the fridge. To use the cream, simply apply a small amount to the affected area and massage it gently. The Piper nigrum in the cream helps to improve blood circulation and reduce numbness in the hands.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, is a popular herb that has been used for centuries to treat various health issues, including numbness in hands. The creams made from rosemary are rich in antioxidants and essential oils that help to improve blood circulation and nerve function. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Piper nigrum herbal creams that help with numbness in hands, you will need some basic ingredients and equipment. First, you will need to dry and grind whole black pepper berries, also known as Piper nigrum, into a fine powder. This can be done by spreading the berries on a baking sheet and placing it in a low-temperature oven (150-200°F) for several hours. Once the powder is ready, you can mix it with a carrier oil such as coconut oil or olive oil in a ratio of 1:10 (one part powder to ten parts oil). Heat the mixture gently in a double boiler or a saucepan over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the powder is fully dissolved. Next, you will need to add a thickening agent such as beeswax or shea butter to the mixture to give it a creamy texture. Stir well and let the mixture cool and thicken before transferring it to a jar or container. Finally, apply a small amount of the cream to your hands and massage it in, focusing on the affected areas. The capsaicin in the black pepper powder can help increase blood flow and reduce numbness in your hands, providing relief and comfort.

You can also add other herbs like turmeric and cinnamon for their anti-inflammatory properties. After mixing, transfer the mixture to a small saucepan and heat it gently over low heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and forms a smooth paste. remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana cream is a natural remedy that can help with numbness in hands. This is because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and ease pain. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Piper nigrum herbal creams that help with numbness in hands, you'll need a few simple ingredients. First, you'll need dried Piper nigrum, also known as black pepper, which is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce numbness and pain in the hands. Start by mixing 2-3 teaspoons of dried Piper nigrum with 1 cup of coconut oil or any other carrier oil in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the oil has absorbed the properties of the Piper nigrum and has a warm, slightly spicy smell. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool down. Once it's cooled, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Add 2-3 tablespoons of beeswax and 2-3 tablespoons of shea butter to the oil mixture and stir until they're fully incorporated. Pour the mixture into small containers or jars and let it cool and solidify. Once it's solid, your Piper nigrum herbal cream is ready to use. Apply a small amount to your hands, massage it in, and let it absorb before washing your hands. Use this cream regularly to help reduce numbness and pain in your hands.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Piper nigrum herbal creams that help with numbness in hands, start by gathering the necessary ingredients, including 2 tablespoons of dried Piper nigrum leaves, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 10 drops of peppermint essential oil. Begin by infusing the dried Piper nigrum leaves in the coconut oil by heating them together in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water for about 2-3 hours, or until the oil has turned green and has a strong peppery aroma. Next, strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, and discard the solids. In a separate bowl, melt the beeswax and shea butter in the microwave or in a double boiler, then mix them together with the infused oil and peppermint essential oil. Pour the mixture into a small tin or container, and let it cool and harden completely. Once hardened, your Piper nigrum herbal cream is ready to use - simply apply it to your hands and massage it in to help alleviate numbness and tingling sensations.

intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great solution for numbness in hands. Numbness in hands can be caused by poor circulation, nerve damage, or even repetitive strain injuries. If left untreated, it can lead to limited mobility, reduced dexterity, and even depression. But what can be done? One option is to use herbal creams that contain Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help improve blood flow to the affected area, reducing numbness and pain. It also has antioxidant properties that can help protect the nerves from damage.  Another herb that can be beneficial is Capsicum annuum, also known as cayenne pepper. Cayenne pepper contains a compound called capsaicin, which can help increase blood flow to the hands and reduce pain. Capsaicin can also help block pain signals to the brain, providing relief from numbness and discomfort.  Using herbal creams that contain these herbs can be a great way to address numbness in hands. These creams can be applied directly to the affected area, providing fast and effective relief. They are also a natural and non-invasive solution, making them a great alternative to medication or surgery. By using herbal creams, you can improve your quality of life and regain control over your hands. You can do everyday activities with ease, like cooking, writing, or even playing music. By addressing numbness in hands, you can also reduce the risk of developing more serious conditions, such as carpal tunnel syndrome or peripheral neuropathy.
supplementary_best: A combination of herbal creams using Zingiber officinale (ginger), Piper nigrum (black pepper), and Capsicum annuum (cayenne pepper) may be effective in relieving numbness in hands. Ginger contains compounds that improve blood circulation, helping to reduce numbness and tingling sensations. Black pepper, on the other hand, contains piperine, a natural anti-inflammatory that helps to reduce swelling and pain. Capsicum annuum's capsaicin is a natural vasodilator that increases blood flow to the hands, reducing numbness and promoting healing. This combination works well together because ginger and black pepper enhance the effects of capsaicin, while capsaicin helps to increase the absorption of ginger and black pepper's active compounds into the skin.
supplementary_avoid: If you have numbness in your hands, it's best to avoid herbal creams containing certain herbs that might make numbness worse. For example, you should avoid creams with 'Valeriana officinalis' as it can cause drowsiness and sedation, which might not be suitable for numbness that needs to be addressed.  You should also avoid creams with 'Glycyrrhiza glabra' as it can cause high blood pressure and heart problems, which might worsen numbness in the hands. Additionally, creams with 'Vitex agnus-castus' might not be suitable as it can cause dizziness and lightheadedness, which could exacerbate numbness.  Another herb to be cautious of is 'Lavandula angustifolia', which can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some people. This could make numbness in the hands worse or lead to other complications.  Lastly, creams with 'Melissa officinalis' should be avoided as they might cause skin sensitivity, which could worsen numbness in the hands.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aromatherapy can help with numbness in hands by inhaling essential oils that improve blood flow and reduce stress. These oils, such as peppermint and eucalyptus, are inhaled through the nose and travel to the brain, where they send signals to increase blood flow to the hands. This increased blood flow helps to reduce numbness and tingling.  Cold or warm compresses can also be used to increase blood flow to the hands. Applying a cold compress can help to constrict blood vessels and then increase blood flow when the compress is removed. This can help to reduce numbness and pain. Applying a warm compress, on the other hand, can help to increase blood flow directly to the hands, reducing numbness and pain.  Exercise, such as hand stretches and finger bends, can also help to increase blood flow to the hands. When we exercise our hands, we cause our blood vessels to dilate, or get wider, which allows more blood to flow to the hands, reducing numbness and tingling.  Acupuncture is another natural remedy that can help with numbness in hands. This involves inserting thin needles into specific points on the body, which can help to increase blood flow to the hands and reduce numbness and pain.  Massage can also help to increase blood flow to the hands by applying pressure to specific points on the body. This pressure causes our blood vessels to dilate, allowing more blood to flow to the hands and reducing numbness and tingling.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience numbness in their hands may also suffer from other conditions that can be alleviated with medicinal herbs. One common issue is tingling or prickling sensations, often referred to as paresthesia. This can be caused by nerve damage or compression, which can be treated with herbs like ginseng and ashwagandha that have anti-inflammatory properties. These herbs help reduce swelling and promote blood flow to the affected area, relieving numbness and tingling.  Another condition associated with numbness in the hands is carpal tunnel syndrome, which occurs when the median nerve in the wrist is compressed. This can lead to pain, numbness, and weakness in the hand and arm. Medicinal herbs like turmeric and ginger have anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and ease pressure on the median nerve, providing relief from numbness and pain.  Additionally, people with numbness in their hands may also experience Raynaud's disease, a condition that affects blood flow to the hands and feet. This can be triggered by cold temperatures or stress, causing the blood vessels to constrict and leading to numbness, pain, and discoloration. Herbs like ginkgo biloba and cayenne pepper can help dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing the frequency and severity of numbness and pain episodes.  Furthermore, numbness in the hands can be a symptom of vitamin deficiencies, particularly vitamin B12 deficiency. This can be treated with herbs like black cumin and bladderwrack, which are rich in vitamin B12 and other essential nutrients. These herbs help replenish the body's stores of vitamin B12 and support nerve function, alleviating numbness and other symptoms of deficiency.
intro_image_alt: herbal creams for numbness in hands
